DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JARYN N. CHANDLER OF LEWIS BRISBOIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-6639 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s demurrer to the second cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Samuel Chandler and Gina O'Reilly is sustained without leave to amend. The applicable test to determine unfairness for a UCL unfair prong claim alleged by a plaintiff who is not a direct competitor of the defendant is currently unsettled and awaits a definitive decision of the California Supreme Court. However, based on a review of the published court of appeal decisions on this issue, the prevailing view is that an unfair prong claim alleged by someone who is not a direct competitor must, at a minimum, allege facts that the assertedly unfair practice in some manner runs afoul of the policy or spirit underlying a constitutional, statutory or regulatory provision. (See generally Graham v. Bank of America, N.A. (2014) 226 Cal. App. 4th 594, 610-614). Plaintiffs in this case make no such allegation, nor does it appear reasonably possible that they can do so. To the contrary, by enacting Vehicle Code 23123 and 23123.5, the Legislature has shown that it is aware of the very problem (distracted driving as a result of smartphone technology in a vehicle) that is the linchpin of plaintiffs' second cause of action, but the Legislature has chosen not to make illegal what plaintiffs in this lawsuit allege is unfair. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JARYN N. CHANDLER OF LEWIS BRISBOIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-6639 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LYFT, INC's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT - Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Samuel Chandler and Gina O'Reilly is granted. Plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts to show that Mr. Strickland acted despicably. While use of a handheld device while driving might in some circumstances be despicable conduct supporting punitive damages liability, it is not in all circumstances. If and when plaintiffs learn additional facts about the manner in which Mr. Strickland used the handheld device and the connection of that use to their claimed injuries, they may bring a motion for leave to file an amended complaint alleging a prayer for punitive damages. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
